page: Microsoft Azure E104i_v5 is an isolated virtual machine instance belonging to the EIv5 server family, running on the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features an x86_64 architecture powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor operating at 2.8 GHz. The hardware configuration delivers 104 vCPUs across 52 physical cores with hyper-threading enabled, supported by 672.0 GB of system memory, translating to 6.46 GB of RAM per core. This instance does not include local storage or onboard GPU accelerators.

Benchmark evaluations indicate strong multi-core performance and memory throughput. The instance achieved a stress-ng multi-core CPU score of 100,833.46 and a Geekbench multi-core score of 17,815.0, both falling into the top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth is highly efficient, with read-only small block cached speeds reaching 5,458.86 GB/sec and uncached large block read speeds at 334.7 GB/sec. Cryptographic capabilities are notable, as evidenced by a top-tier Passmark CPU encryption test score of 93,156.86. However, single-core performance remains average, with a Geekbench single-core score of 1,708.0.

Given its high memory capacity and strong multi-threaded capabilities, the E104i_v5 is optimized for memory-intensive workloads such as large-scale database operations, high-throughput web serving, and software compilation. It demonstrates strong performance in Redis SET operations, processing 93.05 million operations per second, and static web serving at 68.22 GB/sec. While it handles prompt processing for small and medium language models efficiently, it is not suited for large-scale LLM text generation due to a weak score of 2.67 tokens per second on 70B models, reflecting the lack of dedicated GPU hardware. The isolated nature of this instance provides dedicated compute resources, offering high resource density and predictable performance for enterprise-grade deployments.
description: Microsoft Azure E104i_v5 is an isolated, memory-optimized virtual machine instance powered by a dedicated Intel Xeon 8370C processor. Operating on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it provides 104 vCPUs across 52 physical cores, 672.0 GB of RAM, and no local storage or GPU accelerators. Benchmarks reveal strong multi-core and memory performance, highlighted by a stress-ng multi-core score of 100,833.46 and uncached memory read bandwidth of 334.7 GB/sec. It also excels in cryptography and database operations, achieving a top-tier Passmark encryption score. While single-core performance is average, its massive memory footprint and high multi-threaded throughput make it highly suitable for memory-intensive enterprise workloads, large databases, Redis deployments, and high-volume static web serving.
og_description: Microsoft Azure E104i_v5 is an isolated virtual machine featuring 104 vCPUs, 672 GB RAM, and an Intel Xeon 8370C processor, delivering strong multi-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure E104i_v5 is an isolated x86_64 instance with 104 vCPUs and 672 GB RAM, optimized for memory-intensive database and enterprise workloads.
tagline: A high-capacity, isolated x86_64 virtual machine delivering massive memory density and strong multi-threaded performance for demanding enterprise database workloads.
bullet_points: 104 vCPUs and 52 physical cores powered by an Intel Xeon 8370C processor
672.0 GB of dedicated system memory optimized for large-scale databases
Strong multi-core performance with a stress-ng score of 100,833.46
High-speed memory bandwidth reaching 334.7 GB/sec for uncached large blocks
Isolated hardware configuration running on Microsoft Hyper-V with no local storage
Best suited for memory-intensive workloads, Redis, and database operations
